summary: Solid contract with 30 criteria covering all 3 features comprehensively. All fixture files from the product spec are accounted for, helper functions are covered individually, and run.sh core behaviors are tested. Three minor gaps noted below are non-blocking for Sprint 1 since the missing behaviors will be exercised naturally in Sprint 2+ when real test files exist.

criteriaAssessment.testability: GOOD. 29 of 30 criteria have unambiguous pass/fail thresholds. C1-05 is slightly vague ('All .bats files under meta-tests/ are discovered') -- the evaluator will need to decide how to verify discovery (e.g., checking bats invocation arguments or output). Acceptable as-is.
criteriaAssessment.completeness: GOOD with minor gaps. All fixtures from the product spec are covered (C1-17 through C1-27). All 7 helper functions are covered (C1-08 through C1-16). run.sh prerequisites, discovery, exit code, filter, and executable permission are covered (C1-02 through C1-07). Three minor spec behaviors are not covered -- see notes.
criteriaAssessment.specificity: GOOD. Criteria specify exact file paths, exact function names, exact field names, and exact expected values. Fixture criteria (C1-17 through C1-27) specify exact JSON structure to verify. No ambiguous 'should work well' language.

notes.issue: No criterion for run.sh printing a summary (total tests, passed, failed, duration). The product spec lists this as a key behavior of F1. However, since Sprint 1 will have zero or minimal test files, summary output is hard to meaningfully test. This can be deferred to Sprint 6 (Edge Cases and Polish).
No criterion for run.sh TTY detection (--tap vs pretty output). The product spec mentions this as a key behavior. Again, this is polish that can be deferred.

C1-06 tests exit 0 on success, but there is no criterion for exit non-zero when tests fail. The spec says 'Exits with non-zero status if any test fails.' Since Sprint 1 only has infrastructure (no intentionally failing tests), this is hard to test now. Could be addressed by creating a temporary failing .bats file during the test, but deferring to Sprint 2+ when real test files exist is reasonable.

All F2 helper function criteria use code-inspection rather than functional testing. This is the weakest form of verification but is justified: bats setup/teardown functions require the bats runtime to execute properly, and Sprint 1 may not have real .bats test files to exercise them through. C1-29 (integration test) provides a functional sanity check. Sprints 2-6 will exercise every helper function through actual test cases.

Test steps say 'Verify it invokes bats with all .bats files found in meta-tests/' without specifying how the evaluator should verify this. Suggestion: check that the bats command output or run.sh stdout mentions each .bats file, or inspect the run.sh source to confirm the discovery glob pattern. Acceptable as-is for the evaluator to determine approach.

outOfScopeCheck: PASS. Out-of-scope list correctly excludes all Sprint 2-6 features (test-utils-pure.bats, test-utils-state.bats, test-git.bats, test-hooks.bats, test-edge-cases.bats). Also correctly excludes CI/CD, performance, and changes to existing code.
regressionCheck: PASS. regressionSprints is correctly empty -- Sprint 1 has no prior sprints to regress against.
recommendation: Proceed to implementation. The three minor gaps (summary output, TTY detection, exit-non-zero on failure) are reasonable deferrals for a foundation sprint. The evaluator should use judgment on C1-05 verification approach.
